name: gget
tagline: CLI/Python reference for querying 20+ bioinformatics databases (Ensembl, BLAST, AlphaFold, COSMIC, CELLxGENE) via the gget package.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped reference doc for the real gget bioinformatics tool. It explicitly warns against hardcoding COSMIC/OpenAI credentials, recommends env vars, and flags heavy-download operations (AlphaFold ~4GB, gget virus bulk downloads). Main issue: the SKILL.md is ~973 lines, roughly double the recommended size budget, so it eats context even though the c

highlights.summary: Description promises 'gene info, BLAST/BLAT, viral sequence downloads, AlphaFold structures, enrichment analysis, OpenTargets, COSMIC, CELLxGENE, and 8cube' but the body also documents `gget gpt` (Ope
`gget setup alphafold` 'Installs modified third-party dependencies and downloads model parameters' (line 317) without specifying source/checksum verification; matches known real gget behavior but the
At ~973 lines the file is dense; a chunk of parameter-level detail could have been pushed to the referenced references/module_reference.md to keep the main context leaner.
